description:
WHY: "error: not initialized. Run \`bl init\`" is a catchall for at least four distinct failures, all reported identically:
  1. cwd is not in any git repo and no .balls/ found walking up to /
  2. cwd is in a git repo but main_root has no .balls/
  3. cwd is in a non-stealth setup but the state worktree's tasks dir is missing
  4. (currently) cwd is in a bare repo — see the separate discovery bug ticket

For an agent dropped into an unfamiliar repo, "is this an uninitialized sub-project, or am I in the wrong directory entirely?" is the actual question. The current message answers neither. The user's specific suggestion: a line like \`searched: /home/mark/dev/.../current → /home/mark → /\` resolves the wrong-directory case instantly.

WHAT: each NotInitialized site (store.rs:72, 76, 86; store_paths.rs:57; config.rs:85) should produce a distinct, descriptive error. Carry the search path through find_balls_root rather than discarding it. Distinguish "no .balls/ anywhere on the walk" from "found .balls/ at <path> but state worktree missing/broken" from "found .balls/ but cwd resolution mismatched".

CONTEXT: surfaced alongside the bare-repo discovery bug — the misleading error is what hid the real bug during debugging. Improving the message would have shortened diagnosis from minutes to seconds.
title: Improve discovery error messages — distinguish init states and show search path
